auth.title: Auth & SSO
auth.description: Enable Identity Provider, configure OpenID or Entra and control whether SSO should be required.
auth.pageDescription: Select Identity Provider, follow the guide and control whether SSO should be required for the organization.
auth.noActiveOrg: You need to select an active organization before you can configure SSO.

idp.title: Identity Provider
idp.description: Select which IdP should be active and fill in OIDC details. The organization can only have one IdP at a time.
idp.redirectUriHint: Fill in the information according to the guide. Redirect URL for this organization:

wantAssertionsSigned.title: Require signed assertions
wantAssertionsSigned.description: Ensures that IdP signs the responses.

requireSsoDisabled.noIdp: Configure an IdP before SSO can be required.
requireSsoDisabled.incomplete: Fill in all required IdP fields to be able to require SSO.

validation.incomplete: Fill in all required IdP fields before saving.

steps.0: Create a new OIDC app in your IdP (e.g. OneLogin) and select Web client.
steps.1: Set {redirect} as Redirect URL in your IdP.
steps.2: Copy Issuer/Discovery URL, Client ID and Client Secret from the IdP.
steps.3: Fill in these values in CloudPortal under Auth settings for your organization.
steps.4: Test the login before enabling "Require SSO" in CloudPortal.

steps.0: Register a new Entra app (App registration) and select Web as platform.
steps.1: Set Redirect URI to {redirect} in Microsoft Entra ID.
steps.2: Note Application (client) ID, Directory (tenant) ID and create a Client secret.
steps.3: Fill in these values in CloudPortal under Auth settings for your organization.
steps.4: Assign users and test the login, then fill in Tenant ID, Client ID/Secret here before requiring SSO.

steps.0: Create a new SAML app in your IdP and set ACS/Callback URL to the redirect URL above.
steps.1: Copy Issuer/Entity ID, Entry Point (SSO URL) and public X.509 certificate from the IdP.
steps.2: Fill in these values in CloudPortal under Auth settings for your organization.
steps.3: Enter any Audience/Entity ID expected by the IdP and select whether AuthnRequests should be signed.
steps.4: Test the login before enabling "Require SSO" in CloudPortal.

requireSso.title: Require SSO
requireSso.description: All users must log in via selected IdP.

allowLocalLogin.title: Allow local login for owners
allowLocalLogin.description: Keep fallback via password for owners.

features.idp: Select IdP type (OpenID or Entra AD)
features.cloudflare: Follow the guide to configure Cloudflare Access
features.require: Enable "Require SSO" when IdP is ready

email.title: Email settings
email.description: Set up the organization's own SMTP or Graph provider, including branding and test sending.

email.activeOrg: Active organization: {orgName}. All sending (invitations, reports, etc.) uses these settings when override is active.
email.inherited: Email settings are inherited from a higher level
email.inheritedDescription: If no own settings are enabled, settings from provider → distributor → global are used.

form.description: Enter sender address, reply email and provider that should apply for sending.

emailDarkMode.label: Dark mode (default is light mode)
emailDarkMode.description: In dark mode, dark colors are used for email content and background. The background behind the logo uses the NavBar color from branding.

provider.title: Provider
provider.description: Select SMTP or Microsoft Graph and fill in necessary values.

disclaimer.title: Custom disclaimer
disclaimer.description: The text is added at the bottom of all sending (even if email provider is inherited). Markdown is supported for links, lists and emphasis.
disclaimer.emptyHint: Leave empty to use the chain's default text.

testEmail.reconnectAlert: Enter a test recipient to reconnect.

features.override: Enable override for sending within the organization
features.configure: Configure SMTP with or without auth, or Microsoft Graph
features.customize: Customize logo, colors and test run directly from the form

distributor.description: Deletion removes the distributor and all linked data. The distributor can only be deleted if all organizations linked via providers are first removed. This cannot be undone.
distributor.extra: The distributor can only be deleted if all organizations linked via providers are first removed.
distributor.acknowledgement: I understand that the distributor and all linked data (provider links, memberships, etc.) will be permanently deleted. The distributor can only be deleted if all organizations linked via providers are first removed.

provider.description: Deletion removes the provider and all linked data. The provider can only be deleted if all organizations under it are first removed. This cannot be undone.
provider.extra: The provider can only be deleted if all organizations under it are first removed.
provider.acknowledgement: I understand that the provider and all linked data (organizations, memberships, etc.) will be permanently deleted. The provider can only be deleted if all organizations under it are first removed.

delete.confirmText: Confirm the action by entering {possessive} slug ({slug}) and check that you understand the consequences. {extra}
